snippet.title: WorkingNation Overheard: Diana Spencer on HR catching up to skills-based hiring
snippet.description:
There is more traction for skills-based hiring, but HR has some catching up to do, according to Diana Spencer, executive director, William G. McGowan Charitable Fund.

WorkingNation sat down with Spencer at SHRM 2022 Annual Conference & Expo in New Orleans.

She says skills-based hiring addresses many issues. “It addresses people who are jobless, people who have lived in poverty. It addresses so many things to bring people in. What they're learning is that these people often become very loyal to the company. So, I think that there's a lot of power in it, but it still is developing.”

Additionally, the Fund announced at the SHRM gathering its first-ever McGowan Fund Fellows Program Ethical Leader of the Year Award to Charles F. Lowrey, chairman and CEO at Prudential Financial.

Spencer notes it was important to announce the award winner at the SHRM gathering. “Everything goes through HR and additionally, the role of HR today has never been more important. They are, in large part, establishing the culture of the organization. They're implementing the culture of the leader – the CEO, through their hiring practices, through their expectations, through their employee reviews.”

Learn more about the William G. McGowan Charitable Fund: https://williamgmcgowanfund.org/

Our #WorkingNationOverheard interview series lets you listen in on conversations and discussions about employment and education taking place at major conferences around the country. You can find past installments at https://workingnation.org/overheard/.

Check out what they are saying at the #SHRM22 Annual Conference and Expo in New Orleans: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PL2EXy_Hi93bJy8Kdp68K5jfzXD3f8-urt

You can read more about this year's conference at https://annual.shrm.org/ and learn more about SHRM at https://www.shrm.org/pages/default.aspx.

Follow the conversation on social media: #WorkingNation #WorkingNationOverheard #SHRM22 #CauseTheEffect #futureofwork #workforcedevelopment #skillsgap #jobs #work #career #skills 

For stories about solutions to the jobs skills gap disrupting our economy and the future of work, visit WorkingNation at https://workingnation.org/.

snippet.title: Prudential CEO on Earnings, Russia Volatility

snippet.title: Cyber Spotlight: How to Navigate a Volatile Market
snippet.description:
As recent world events continue to develop and the market experiences an economic downturn, it is imperative that founders learn how to navigate a volatile market. Gain critical advice for entrepreneurs and CEOs from industry experts Jay Leek (Co-Founder & Managing Partner, SYN Ventures), Roger Hale (Former CSO, BigID), and Andy Ellis (Operating Partner, YL Ventures & Advisory CISO, Orca Security).

Check out insightful highlights from our recent Fireside Chat moderated by Managing Partner Yoav Leitersdorf!

snippet.title: Prudential Rob Falzon Millennials, Money, and the New Relationship with Work at CNBC Work Summit
snippet.description:
Having lived and worked through the Great Recession and the pandemic, millennials are bracing for another dose of economic uncertainty. How will they navigate these yet another "once in a generation" event? This session, featuring findings from Prudential Financial’s Pulse survey series, will focus on the largest generation in the U.S. workforce and how setback after setback has shaped their relationship with work, their employers, and their financial security.

Rob Falzon, Prudential Financial Vice Chair
Interviewer: Jen Rogers, Financial Reporter 

More from the CNBC Events: http://www.CNBCEvents.com 
Subscribe to the CNBC Events Marketing Newsletter here: https://bit.ly/3QlH7pO 

Follow on Twitter: https://twitter.com/cnbcevents 
Follow on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/showcase/cnbcevents
#CNBC #Work

snippet.description:

NEW: Everything I learnt helping people get hired since 2013 (and my 80/20 system) in my new book: https://www.amazon.co.uk/gp/product/B0GWTZPMHX

Our small team of 3 have made over 1,000+ free career videos since 2020.

📚Courses to Get You Hired📚

I have also made some high quality courses which are kept up to date.

🔴Worried about your next interview? Try our 2025 No-BS Interview Course. Updated for all the latest practises from large companies including mini case studies, presentations and writing business emails: https://ishortn.ink/3Gmj0nVx2

🔴Got an Assessment Centre coming up? Most people walk into the final stage of a graduate application process with no preparation. Prepare in as little as 3 hours with our “Acing Assessment Course” , includes 20 custom exercises and 100+ walkthrough videos: https://ishortn.ink/EmDlKPKAD 

🔴Obsessively organised and want the best interview preparation possible? Try our Complete Interview Course with almost 5 hours of walkthrough videos, 80+ sample answers and everything you need to prepare for any type of interview: https://ishortn.ink/zQa4IxQQN 

🔴Want to work at PwC, Deloitte, KPMG or EY? We’ve put together a complete “Breaking Into Big 4” course. Containing everything you need to pass the first round interview and ace their assessment centre: https://ishortn.ink/qPoYa9cpx 

✅We’ve got courses to help everyone. You can find all our courses here: https://ishortn.ink/0AhD7AS6i 

📞Want to work with me?📞

Email me directly at mike@jobreadyenglish.com
Send me a contact request on LinkedIn: https://ishortn.ink/Ra8q7ywE3 
Read my personal writing on my website: https://ishortn.ink/FhbWplY3N

snippet.title: Pacific Investment - Company Overview
snippet.description:
We offer a comprehensive range of specialized capabilities that help clients at different stages of their investment journey. We seek to build long-term partnerships with investors, ensuring we fully understand their particular circumstances and requirements managing investments for both individuals and for large institutional investors.

Pacific Investments has a strong commitment to sustainability. Creating lasting positive impact is one of the core principles of our Charter and one that applies to all our activities as a firm. It guides our investment activities, our corporate activities and our daily interactions with all of our stakeholders.

snippet.title: Capital Southwest mit 45% Dividendenwachstum pro Jahr!
snippet.description:
Capital Southwest mit 45% Dividendenwachstum pro Jahr!

Viel Spaß mit meinem Video! 

In diesem Video befassen wir uns mit der Fundamentalanalyse, dem fairen Wert der Aktie und der Charttechnik.

In der Fundamentalanalyse betrachten wir die Passivseite der Bilanz, wie ist das Verhältnis von Schulden zu Eigenkapital und kann das Eigenkapital die Schulden zurückzahlen.

Wir schauen uns auch die Dividendenerhöhung der letzten Jahre an und wie hoch die Eigenkapitalquote ist.

Auf der Gewinn- und Verlustseite der Bilanz sehen wir uns an, was von den Gesamteinnahmen nach Fixkosten und variablen Kosten übrig bleibt.

Wir sehen uns auch an, wie viel Nettoumsatz dem Eigenkapital zugerechnet wird und wie hoch der Kassenbestand am Ende des Geschäftsjahres ist.

Wir werfen auch einen Blick auf den fairen Preis der Aktie.

In der Chartanalyse betrachten wir die Unterstützungs- und Widerstandszonen (Support und Resist).

Dieses Video eignet sich besonders für diejenigen, die passives Einkommen generieren wollen, denn je näher man am fairen Preis kauft, desto höher ist das passive Einkommen, wenn der Einstieg über die Charttechnik erfolgt, wenn man keinen Sparplan hat.
_________________________________________________________________________________

Hier ein paar angesagt YouTube Kanäle:

@MaximilianGamperling 

@Sparkojote 

@finanztip 
_________________________________________________________________________________

Hier die Playlist zu Fundamentalanalyse, fairer Wert bei Aktien und Charttechnik:
https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLyMmTyP1MmzO-p-6D23xM2QY2hUA5yOTm

Hier die Playlist mit den Aktienduellen:
https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLyMmTyP1MmzOvf2JmwCCKQ4T7DTKvONeE

Hier die Playlist zur meiner Einschätzung anhand der Charttechnik bei Aktien, Index, Devisen und Rohstoffe:
https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLyMmTyP1MmzPjYsDOnDKjQLB2QClDw4D3

Abonniere hier kostenlos meinen YouTube Kanal: https://goo.gl/VKCxsy
_________________________________________________________________________________

Risikohinweis:
Ich übernehme keinerlei Haftung dafür, dass die Informationen alle richtig sind, dieses Video dient lediglich zu Informations- und Lehrzwecken.

Der Handel mit Finanzprodukten (wie zum Beispiel Devisen, Differenzkontrakte, Aktien, Optionen, Optionsscheine oder Zertifikate) ist mit einem hohen Risiko verbunden und daher nicht für sicherheitsbewusste Anleger geeignet. 

Totalverlust ist möglich.
Dieses Video ist keine Empfehlung zum Kauf oder Verkauf.

Mit dem kauf von Aktien beteiligt man sich an der positiven wirtschaftlichen Entwicklung eines Unternehmens durch Kurssteigerungen und Dividendenzahlungen, man beteiligt sich auch an der negativen wirtschaftlichen Entwicklung eines Unternehmens durch Kursverluste und einer kompletten Aussetzung der Dividendenzahlung.

Darum ist die Risikostreuung oder Diversifikation sehr wichtig damit das Gesamtrisiko des Portfolios minimiert wird.
_________________________________________________________________________________

Hinweis nach WPHG §34b zur Aufklärung über mögliche Interessenskonflikte:
Der Autor handelt regelmäßig mit den erwähnten Wertpapieren und besitzt eventuell Positionen in den genannten Wertpapieren.
_________________________________________________________________________________

Inhaltsverzeichnis:
00:00 Einleitung
00:33 Capital Southwest mit 45% Dividendenwachstum pro Jahr!

_________________________________________________________________________________

#dividenden #dividendenstrategie #dividendenaktien #fundamentalanalyse #chartanalyse #aktienanalyse #fairerpreis

snippet.title: Händel - Ombra mai fu "Largo from Xerxes"
snippet.description:
Händel - Xerxes - Ombra mai fu

"Ombra mai fu", also known as "Largo from Xerxes", is the opening aria from the 1738 opera Serse by George Frideric Handel.

Georg Friederich Händel born on 23 February 1685 and died on  14 April 1759.
Händel was a German-British Baroque composer becoming well known for his operas, oratorios, anthems, concerti grossi, and organ concertos. Handel received his training in Halle and worked as a composer in Hamburg and Italy before settling in London in 1712, where he spent the bulk of his career and became a naturalised British subject in 1727. He was strongly influenced both by the middle-German polyphonic choral tradition and by composers of the Italian Baroque.

Handel started three commercial opera companies to supply the English nobility with Italian opera. In 1737 he had a physical breakdown, changed direction creatively, and addressed the middle class and made a transition to English choral works. After his success with Messiah (1742), he never composed an Italian opera again. His orchestral Water Music and Music for the Royal Fireworks remain steadfastly popular. One of his four coronation anthems, Zadok the Priest, has been performed at every British coronation since 1727. Almost blind, he died in 1759, a respected and rich man, and was given a state funeral at Westminster Abbey.

Handel composed more than forty opera serias over a period of more than thirty years. Since the late 1960s, interest in Handel's music has grown. The musicologist Winton Dean wrote that "Handel was not only a great composer; he was a dramatic genius of the first order." His music exerted a strong influence on Classical-era composers, including Mozart and Beethoven.

Lyrics:
Frondi tenere e belle
Del mio platano amato
Per voi risplenda il fato
Tuoni, lampi, e procelle
Non v'oltraggino mai la cara pace
Né giunga a profanarvi austro rapace

Ombra mai fu
Di vegetabile
Cara ed amabile
Soave più
Ombra mai fu
Di vegetabile
Cara ed amabile
Soave più
Cara ed amabile
Ombra mai fu
Di vegetabile
Cara ed amabile
Soave più
Soave più

Enjoy!

snippet.title: Radically Happy | Summary In Under 11 Minutes (Book by Phakchok Rinpoche and Erric Solomon)
snippet.description:
Almost everyone in the world desires happiness but many of us are pretty bad at figuring out what would really make us happy. We look for happiness in things, like houses or cars, or having that beautiful job, but does these actually make us happy? How can we know what really makes us happy?
************
Our Recommended Video List for Business, Money and Self Help:

1. The 5 AM Club (By Robin Sharma) - https://youtu.be/fOpqQ2PK-iE
2. How to Decide (By Annie Duke) - https://youtu.be/vGGdUmzAERw
3. Less Doing, More Living (By Ari Meisel) - https://youtu.be/ban-UbniwS8
4. The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck (By Mark Manson) - https://youtu.be/Zam-jVbJOo4
5. The Power of Habit (By Charles Duhigg) - https://youtu.be/j0cUspzE2lI

************

Timestamps:
00:00 - Intro
00:41 - Genuine happiness has nothing to do with possessions or circumstances
01:28 - The first step toward radical happiness is breaking the cycle of thinking about thoughts
02:56 - You can placate your mind by practicing meditating with an object
03:58 - To achieve basic happiness, practice cultivating gratitude instead of comparing yourself to other people
05:06 - Throughout the day, take mindful mini-breaks to refocus on the present moment
05:55 - Interconnected happiness means living in harmony with those around us
06:33 - Cultivate warm relationships by appreciating interconnectedness and wishing kindness on everyone
07:49 -Extend your feelings of warmth to everyone
08:25 - Radical happiness comes with greater understanding and awareness of your own mind
09:52 - What's your most important key takeaway? 

************
DISCLAIMER: This book summary is meant as a preview and not a replacement for the original work. If you like this summary please consider purchasing the original book to get the full experience as the original author intended it to be. This video is for entertainment purposes only.
